Operations in the orbiter processing facility are limited by access platforms that are limited to loads of 500 pounds or less. Telescopic tubes have experienced binding as they were lowered, indicated by a tube descending suddenly to its proper position after becoming free. Servicing operations utilizing payload bay access platforms have shown that a common speed mode for both east and west platforms is undesirable.
recommendation_text: Design positive lockout devices that will ensure compliance with lockout/tagout directives. Install operational stopping devices that will permit stowing of the platform vertically without the use of the upper limit switch. Momentary switches utilized in control systems should have a manual release capability designed in. Design the wire rope spooling systems to include stops that will prevent excessive amounts of wire rope from being released. Enhance the hoist system of the OPF access platforms to support heavier loads (up to 1500 pounds). Provide a device in the wire rope system that will sense excessive loads and stop the hoist when a safe load is exceeded or the cable is slack. Change the High Bay 1 access platform to operate the same way as High Bay 2, where both east and west platforms have independent speed modes.

evidence.quote: Upper limit switches being used as operational stops can not be relied upon to stop the operation during an emergency.
Use of an inadequate tagout or lockout device may allow improper restoration of a system previously tagged as hazardous.
Hoist systems (as used in the OPF) can release excessive lengths of cable from the spool.

impact_notes: Platform fell during orbiter closeout operations, damaging left payload bay door and injuring technician. Cable failure resulted from inadequate lockout compliance and design deficiencies in hoist system.

title: Crane Load Cell System
driving_event_text: On November 8, 1985, the forward center segment of a solid rocket motor (SRM) was damaged during forward handling ring removal operations. During the operation leading to this incident, the crane operator was attempting to raise the hook until a load of 11,000 pounds was attained, but the load cell read zero until the segmented ring fractured and the crane was stopped. The load cell system was provided with the crane, but the requirement for its usage is not clear. Loadcell usage is not mentioned in the crane OMRSD nor is it mentioned in the system assurance analysis (SAA).
lesson_text: Failure of a crane load cell system during lifting operations contributed to the damage of a solid rocket motor segment.
recommendation_text: Institute/write procedures for the operation, maintenance, and calibration of load cell usage.

evidence.quote: the crane operator was attempting to raise the hook until a load of 11,000 pounds was attained, but the load cell read zero
the requirement for its usage is not clear. Loadcell usage is not mentioned in the crane OMRSD nor is it mentioned in the system assurance analysis
Institute/write procedures for the operation, maintenance, and calibration of load cell usage

impact_notes: Forward center segment of solid rocket motor fractured during handling operations, resulting in hardware damage and operational delay.

title: LRU Platform Hoist and Tube Assembly; Platform Hoist Tube Assembly "UP" Limit Switch Bypass
driving_event_text: Three sections of a telescoping tube assembly at Pad B rotating service structure (RSS) fell while being extended to incorporate a planned modification (MOD). The MOD intended to eliminate binding that had been experienced during testing and verification. The falling tubes impacted and severely damaged the RSS and orbital maneuvering system pod covers. The contributing factor to this failure is that the fillet welds on tube "D" provided an outward ramp effect that caused excessive wear on the keepers and the eventual bowing of tube "C" which allowed less surface contact area between the welded stops and the keepers.
lesson_text: Excessive wear of the load bearing surfaces on the keepers of tube "C" was the result of impact during approximately 30 retraction cycles. To stow the tubes, normal operations required bypassing the "up" limit switch and using observers to determine when to stop the upward travel.
recommendation_text: Design tube hoist assemblies that do not incorporate fillet welds on the inside of the tubes and opposite the tubes stops. This will reduce/eliminate wearing down of the tube stops. Add "up" limit switches exclusively dedicated to tube stowage operations.

evidence.quote: fillet welds on tube "D" provided an outward ramp effect that caused excessive wear on the keepers and the eventual bowing of tube "C"
normal operations required bypassing the "up" limit switch and using observers to determine when to stop the upward travel
Excessive wear of the load bearing surfaces on the keepers of tube "C" was the result of impact during approximately 30 retraction cycles

impact_notes: Three sections of telescoping tube assembly fell, severely damaging the rotating service structure and orbital maneuvering system pod covers.

title: Emergency Stop (E-STOP) Switch Cover; Inadvertent Disconnection of Flex Hoses; Orbiter Tow Tug and Support Truck Emergency Stop
driving_event_text: After the towing convoy turned off the runway onto the tow way, the cool tractor/trailer suddenly stopped. The observer caused the rig to suddenly stop when his hand inadvertently came in contact with the E-STOP switch cover. This caused the boom to oscillate up and down. The boom operator lowered the boom to get maximum extension on the two coolant flex hoses attached to the orbiter. The boom's oscillation caused the flex hoses to be whipped loose before the orbiter could stop. The flex hoses fell to the ground with freon 14 spewing from them.
lesson_text: Emergency stop switch covers made of soft or pliable material allowing inadvertent actuation of emergency stop switch. When the trailing support vehicle inadvertently stops, the tow tug continues allowing the flex hoses to pull free and spew freon 14. Lack of integrated emergency stop control switches allowed the tow tug to proceed when the trailing support vehicle initiated an emergency stop.
recommendation_text: Utilize double action switches for emergency stop switches were there is a possibility of inadvertent actuation. Add automatic cut off switch activated by a flow sensor, that will stop the flow of freon during unplanned flex hose separations. During tow operations the dead-man switches of both the tow tug and the support truck should be wired together so both trucks will stop together.

evidence.quote: Emergency stop switch covers made of soft or pliable material allowing inadvertent actuation of emergency stop switch.
Lack of integrated emergency stop control switches allowed the tow tug to proceed when the trailing support vehicle initiated an emergency stop.
the observer caused the rig to suddenly stop when his hand inadvertently came in contact with the E-STOP switch cover

impact_notes: Loss of freon 14 coolant from orbiter support system; environmental release; potential damage to orbiter thermal management systems and operational delay.

title: Unprotected Auxiliary Light Sources
driving_event_text: The Hopkins University Telescope (HUT) experiment was being aligned in the operations and check-out building using a bright light that had been obtained from technicolor government services. After approximately two hours of use without incident, the light bulb shattered, with pieces falling into the HUT experiment, causing contamination. Alignment of the HUT was not a scheduled activity at KSC. Therefore a light source was not furnished with the experiment. Data recorded at KSC indicated that the HUT was not aligned to the cruciform as expected and that a light source would be required.
lesson_text: Using an unprotected external high-intensity lamp for illumination above sensitive flight hardware resulted in damage when the lamp exploded.
recommendation_text: Lead engineers should approve only protected light sources when there is a potential for damage to flight hardware.

evidence.quote: Using an unprotected external high-intensity lamp for illumination above sensitive flight hardware resulted in damage when the lamp exploded.
Alignment of the HUT was not a scheduled activity at KSC. Therefore a light source was not furnished with the experiment.
Lead engineers should approve only protected light sources when there is a potential for damage to flight hardware.

impact_notes: Flight hardware (HUT experiment) contaminated by shattered lamp bulb fragments, requiring re-alignment and rework.

title: Explosion Proof AC Connectors
driving_event_text: Employees were checking for correct phase rotation on cross-hinds AC receptacle. One employee attempted to manually twist a receptacle with a screwdriver. During this action the receptacle faulted either phase to phase or phase to ground. This electrical fault caused an arc flash burn to both employees' eyes.
lesson_text: An explosion proof connector inadvertently faulted, causing an arc flashburn to the technicians' eyes.
recommendation_text: Develop a test plan to study these connectors to determine the useful life expectancy (MTBF); then replace them before a more severe mishap occurs.

evidence.quote: An explosion proof connector inadvertently faulted, causing an arc flashburn to the technicians' eyes.
Develop a test plan to study these connectors to determine the useful life expectancy (MTBF); then replace them before a more severe mishap occurs.
One employee attempted to manually twist a receptacle with a screwdriver. During this action the receptacle faulted either phase to phase or phase to ground.

impact_notes: Arc flash burn injuries to both technicians' eyes; potential for more severe mishap if connector failure not addressed through MTBF testing and replacement program.

title: Fork-lift Crown Manaboard Steering Control Electronics
driving_event_text: Materials were being pulled for issue at the logistics facility using a fork-lift crown manaboard. The operator had already made several issues and was in the process of returning materials to their location. He was looking at the location of his next issue and when he looked up the manaboard was heading for the racks. He immediately hit the brakes but it was too late, the fork-lift hit the shelving support structure and damaged it.
lesson_text: Inadvertent malfunction of crown manaboards steering control electronics, caused the crown manaboard to jump off the control track.
recommendation_text: Redesign the crown manaboard steering control electronics unit to reduce the possibility of future mishaps.

evidence.quote: Inadvertent malfunction of crown manaboards steering control electronics, caused the crown manaboard to jump off the control track.
Redesign the crown manaboard steering control electronics unit to reduce the possibility of future mishaps.

The operator had already made several issues and was in the process of returning materials to their location. He was looking at the location of his next issue and when he looked up the manaboard was heading for the racks.

impact_notes: Facility damage to shelving support structure; operational disruption to logistics materials handling; potential safety risk to personnel from equipment malfunction.

title: Thermal Protection System Components
driving_event_text: While performing an area walkdown, a supervisor, noticed what looked like paint chips on the pick board in the forward RCS area. After further investigation it was determined that air flowing from a nearby duct was blowing against a thermal protection system (TPS) blanket causing irreparable damage.
lesson_text: Incomplete documentation and instructions resulted in one of the orbiter's insulation blankets being damaged by air flow from an ECS duct.
recommendation_text: Provide appropriate inputs to applicable standard practice instructions and work authorizing documents.

evidence.quote: Incomplete documentation and instructions resulted in one of the orbiter's insulation blankets being damaged by air flow from an ECS duct.
air flowing from a nearby duct was blowing against a thermal protection system (TPS) blanket causing irreparable damage
Provide appropriate inputs to applicable standard practice instructions and work authorizing documents.

impact_notes: Thermal protection system blanket sustained irreparable damage requiring replacement; potential safety risk if TPS integrity compromised on orbiter.

title: OMS/RCS System Control Logic
driving_event_text: While pressurizing the oxidizer propellant tank, a change in ullage pressure of 8 psi occurred and caused the computer program to indicate that the interface and supply valves were closed. At this point, the engineer felt it was safe to close the dome load valve, in reality opening it. He then opened the interface valve, the pressure online with the module was 190 psig, the supply valve was then opened and the pressure spiked to approximately 400 psig, which ruptured the burst disk. The computer program indicated the incorrect status of the system because the control logic limits were not adjusted/reset prior to starting the operation.
lesson_text: OMS/RCS Systems program control logic does not reset each time the program is activated, allowing inadvertent over-pressurization of the system.
recommendation_text: Add a step to all OMS/RCS system OMI's that are monitored by control logic, to adjust logic limits prior to changing pressure.

evidence.quote: OMS/RCS Systems program control logic does not reset each time the program is activated, allowing inadvertent over-pressurization of the system.
The computer program indicated the incorrect status of the system because the control logic limits were not adjusted/reset prior to starting the operation.
Add a step to all OMS/RCS system OMI's that are monitored by control logic, to adjust logic limits prior to changing pressure.

impact_notes: Burst disk rupture due to pressure spike to ~400 psig; system over-pressurization hazard with potential for propellant tank failure and mission-critical OMS/RCS system loss.

title: Insulation Baking Process for RCS Thrusters
driving_event_text: Three RCS primary thruster injector/chamber assemblies were damaged when the oven temperature control system malfunctioned and permitted the temperature to reach an estimated 1400 deg F during a cerachrome insulation baking process that should have been limited to 625 deg F. These units should have been rejected at this point, but quality assurance was not notified. As a result, the full extent of damage was not determined until the severe discoloration of titanium alloy and columbium parts was noted during the post-baking weighing process, about three weeks later.
lesson_text: Lack of redundant high temperature cut-off switches allowed 3 RCS thrusters to be over heated during insulation baking process.
recommendation_text: Install redundant separate high temperature cut-offs on the oven power supply with appropriate alarm systems.

evidence.quote: Lack of redundant high temperature cut-off switches allowed 3 RCS thrusters to be over heated during insulation baking process.

quality assurance was not notified. As a result, the full extent of damage was not determined until the severe discoloration of titanium alloy and columbium parts was noted during the post-baking weighing process, about three weeks later.
oven temperature control system malfunctioned and permitted the temperature to reach an estimated 1400 deg F during a cerachrome insulation baking process that should have been limited to 625 deg F.

impact_notes: Three RCS primary thruster injector/chamber assemblies damaged; titanium alloy and columbium parts severely discolored; three-week delay in damage discovery.

title: SRB Holddown Stud Detensioning Operation
driving_event_text: During detensioning operations of a holddown post, while pressure of approximately 8K to 10K lbs was being applied, a loud "bang" was heard. Pressure on the tensioner fell to zero. It was later determined that the loud "bang" was the result of thread failure between the puller bar socket and the SRB holddown stud. With only 8 threads engaged, the shear stresses increased to 96,000 psi, as compared to 98,000 psi shear strength of the stud. Examination of the holddown stud revealed that 8 threads sheared on a 135 degree circumferential segment of the holddown stud.
lesson_text: Failure to fully engage the puller bar prior to detensioning a holddown post, resulted in failure of the puller bar socket-to-holddown post threads.
recommendation_text: Include a requirement in the OMI to take a measurement verifying that the puller bar is fully engaged prior to start of detensioning operation.

evidence.quote: Failure to fully engage the puller bar prior to detensioning a holddown post, resulted in failure of the puller bar socket-to-holddown post threads.
With only 8 threads engaged, the shear stresses increased to 96,000 psi, as compared to 98,000 psi shear strength of the stud.
Include a requirement in the OMI to take a measurement verifying that the puller bar is fully engaged prior to start of detensioning operation.

impact_notes: Hardware damage to SRB holddown stud requiring replacement; operational delay during detensioning; potential safety risk if stud failure had occurred under different load conditions.

title: Ground Wire Connections
driving_event_text: A General Contractor, was in the process of installing a hydraulic elevator at the centaur payload operations control center, (CPOCC) Phase I, Cape Canaveral Air Force Station, when a technician was electrocuted. The electrocution was caused by the victim's own failure to follow safe grounding procedures. For some unknown reason the victim failed to connect the ground wire in the machine room controller. The victim was familiar and educated to the proper safety precautions.
lesson_text: Failure to connect elevator car ground wire in the machine room controller resulted in injury to personnel.
recommendation_text: Train technicians to check for and establish a path to ground prior to and after maintenance operations.

evidence.quote: The victim was familiar and educated to the proper safety precautions...for some unknown reason the victim failed to connect the ground wire
Train technicians to check for and establish a path to ground prior to and after maintenance operations

impact_notes: Personnel electrocution injury during elevator installation at Cape Canaveral facility.

title: Dynatube Leak Checks
driving_event_text: While dynatube leak checks were completed, manifolds were vented to ambient pressure. This allowed thrusters to leak. After the third flow, engineer positioned ullage valve (V4) to closed, this caused a pressure spike that ruptured the valve burst disk. The engineer was unaware of the failure, which was found during subsequent testing and data review.
lesson_text: Inadvertent actuation of the ullage valve resulted in a valve burst disk failure and oxidizer vapor release.
recommendation_text: Put indications in the operating instructions of exact operation end point, and require safety buy-off to ensure compliance.

evidence.quote: engineer positioned ullage valve (V4) to closed, this caused a pressure spike that ruptured the valve burst disk. The engineer was unaware of the failure
Put indications in the operating instructions of exact operation end point, and require safety buy-off to ensure compliance
manifolds were vented to ambient pressure. This allowed thrusters to leak

impact_notes: Valve burst disk failure with oxidizer vapor release; failure discovered post-event during testing and data review rather than during operations.

title: Portable Purge Unit (PPU) Exceeds Recommended Noise Levels
driving_event_text: The portable purge unit when operating, exceeds the recommended 85 DBA. Reference is made to noise hazard survey accomplished by BOC industrial hygiene. The noise level closer than a perimeter of 30 feet exceeds the allowable 85.0 DBA as established by the NASA Hearing Conservation Program. The DBA level near the unit ranges from 91.0 DBA to 99.6 DBA.
lesson_text: A noise survey performed by the BOC industrial hygiene office revealed that the noise level of the PPU when operating exceeds the recommended 85 DBA.
recommendation_text: Ensure the operations instruction document requires the use of hearing protection prior to start up of the PPU.

evidence.quote: noise level of the PPU when operating exceeds the recommended 85 DBA...DBA level near the unit ranges from 91.0 DBA to 99.6 DBA
Ensure the operations instruction document requires the use of hearing protection prior to start up of the PPU

impact_notes: Occupational health hazard: noise exposure 6–15 dB above safe limits within 30-foot operational perimeter poses hearing damage risk to personnel without proper controls.

title: Environmental Control System (ECS) Purge of Orbiter Compartments
driving_event_text: The OPF environmental control system (ECS) supplies conditioned air through ducts to the orbiter to purge and pressurize the forward/aft compartments and the payload bay. Over-pressurization could occur during ECS purge if the ventdoors are closed. No automatic monitoring and/or control exists to preclude simultaneous application of ECS purge air while all orbiter vent doors are closed, or to automatically relieve excess pressure under these circumstances.
lesson_text: Inadvertent closure of the vent doors during environmental control system (ECS) purge of orbiter compartments will result in over-pressurization.
recommendation_text: Require aproprieate operations instructions to verify that all orbiter vent doors are in the standard purge configuration prior to start of venting.

evidence.quote: No automatic monitoring and/or control exists to preclude simultaneous application of ECS purge air while all orbiter vent doors are closed
Require appropriate operations instructions to verify that all orbiter vent doors are in the standard purge configuration prior to start of venting
Inadvertent closure of the vent doors during environmental control system (ECS) purge of orbiter compartments will result in over-pressurization

impact_notes: Over-pressurization of orbiter compartments could damage equipment, structures, or systems; potential safety risk to vehicle and personnel during ground operations.

title: Maintenance of Environmental Control System (ECS) fans
driving_event_text: The fans are located on the 107 foot level of the rotating service structure (RSS) and controlled from the computer console for ECS in the PTCR. The circuit breakers providing power to the fans are located in the hoist room on the 207 foot level of the RSS; therefore, there is no local control. When maintenance personnel are working on the fans, it is possible that someone could re-apply power to them. Also, if local switches were located immediately outside the locked fan rooms, should a problem arise with the fans that generated excessive noise, smoke, etc. They could be shut down by personnel and reduce damage.
lesson_text: Inadvertent actuation of ECS fans S1 and S2 at the payload changeout room (PCR) during maintenance will cause injury or death of personnel.
recommendation_text: Add a requirement to the operations instruction manual requiring the technicians to lock out electrical power prior to beginning maintenance.

evidence.quote: When maintenance personnel are working on the fans, it is possible that someone could re-apply power to them.
Inadvertent actuation of ECS fans S1 and S2 at the payload changeout room (PCR) during maintenance will cause injury or death of personnel.
Add a requirement to the operations instruction manual requiring the technicians to lock out electrical power prior to beginning maintenance.

impact_notes: Potential injury or death of maintenance personnel due to inadvertent fan actuation during maintenance operations.

title: Payload Retention Latch Assembly (PRLA)
driving_event_text: OMRSD S073A.90 thru .230 requires verification of voltages, phasing, and talkbacks to the orbiter. Present talkback verification method for the payload retention latch assembly (PRLA) involves extensive "hot" pin jumpering during testing using a 79K07833 breakout box and test leads. Improper jumper procedure by inserting a jumper at an incorrect location could result in inadvertent shorts or cross connection of live 120 volt and 28 volt electrical lines. This improper procedure could cause PRLA electrical system damage, orbiter interface damage and/or electrical shock/personnel injury.
lesson_text: Improper jumper procedures during testing cause shorts or cross connection of live 120 volt and 28 volt electrical lines, damaging orbiter interfaces.
recommendation_text: Design a status tester and jumper verification device to ensure proper jumper connections of the PRLA prior to interfacing with the orbiter.

evidence.quote: Present talkback verification method for the payload retention latch assembly (PRLA) involves extensive "hot" pin jumpering during testing using a 79K07833 breakout box and test leads.
Improper jumper procedure by inserting a jumper at an incorrect location could result in inadvertent shorts or cross connection of live 120 volt and 28 volt electrical lines.
Design a status tester and jumper verification device to ensure proper jumper connections of the PRLA prior to interfacing with the orbiter.

impact_notes: Risk of electrical shock/personnel injury, PRLA electrical system damage, and orbiter interface damage from improper jumper connections during live voltage testing.

title: Orbiter Weather Protection System
driving_event_text: Extension of the -Y curtain wall with the -Y strut in any position except fully extended, the curtain wall could contact the STS vehicle or the RSS structure. This can result from human error or human error coupled with mechanical failure of the key-lock and interlock valves.
lesson_text: When the -Y curtain wall is extended with the -Y strut in any position except fully extended, the wall could contact and damage the STS vehicle.
recommendation_text: Design redundant interlock system to ensure the -Y curtain wall is fully retracted and locked in its support frame prior to moving the -Y strut.

evidence.quote: Extension of the -Y curtain wall with the -Y strut in any position except fully extended, the curtain wall could contact the STS vehicle or the RSS structure. This can result from human error or human error coupled with mechanical failure o
Design redundant interlock system to ensure the -Y curtain wall is fully retracted and locked in its support frame prior to moving the -Y strut.
This can result from human error or human error coupled with mechanical failure of the key-lock and interlock valves.

impact_notes: Potential contact and damage to STS vehicle and RSS structure; safety risk to orbiter and ground support equipment.

title: Orbiter Structural Integrity
driving_event_text: During launch and ascent, ice from the ET, SRBs, and orbiter may be dislodged by ignition vibroacoustic or aerodynamic forces and impact the orbiter windows or thermal protection system (TPS) with sufficient force to dislodge TPS or damage windows.
lesson_text: During launch ice may be dislodged by vibroacoustic or aerodynamic forces and impact the orbiter's windows or TPS with enough force to cause damage.
recommendation_text: Ensure all lines are maintained with proper insulation and all surfaces are visually inspected for excessive ice build-up prior to clear for launch.

evidence.quote: Ensure all lines are maintained with proper insulation and all surfaces are visually inspected for excessive ice build-up prior to clear for launch.
During launch ice may be dislodged by vibroacoustic or aerodynamic forces and impact the orbiter's windows or TPS with enough force to cause damage.

impact_notes: Ice impact on orbiter windows or thermal protection system during ascent poses critical safety risk to vehicle structural integrity and crew safety.

title: Radio Communications During SCAPE Operations
driving_event_text: Personnel in SCAPE are required to have portable backup communications. GP-1098 requires using the buddy system. Also, in the event of loss of RF communications, two people must be designated to observe each other's safety when in a dangerous situation. The system requires a visual contact and a proximity that allows each buddy to help the other in an emergency. When and if communications are lost, the operation shall be terminated immediately and will not resume until communications are restored.
lesson_text: Loss of radio communications during SCAPE operations has resulted in injury to personnel.
recommendation_text: Ensure all personnel involved in SCAPE operations have backup radio communication systems available prior to starting the SCAPE operation.

evidence.quote: Loss of radio communications during SCAPE operations has resulted in injury to personnel.
When and if communications are lost, the operation shall be terminated immediately and will not resume until communications are restored.
Ensure all personnel involved in SCAPE operations have backup radio communication systems available prior to starting the SCAPE operation.

impact_notes: Personnel injury occurred due to loss of radio communications during SCAPE operations.

title: Spent Booster Segment Handling Operation
driving_event_text: During forward center segment on-load to rail car for transport to the furbishment facility, the segment began to slide out of the confines of the handling sling. Approximately 90% of the segment was suspended 10' to 15' feet above the rail car when the aft end of the segment slipped out of the sling, coming to rest on the rail car. The forward end of the segment stayed suspended approximately 10' above the rail car. No damage to the segment was noted and the rail car received only three scratches by the handling ring. The instability of the segment is due to the difficulty in determining its center of gravity.
lesson_text: Determining the center of gravity of spent booster segments prior to a lift operation is very difficult, often allowing dangerous conditions to exist.
recommendation_text: Design a handling sling device that will self-adjust to the load as it is lifted and keep it steady throughout the lifting operation.

evidence.quote: Determining the center of gravity of spent booster segments prior to a lift operation is very difficult, often allowing dangerous conditions to exist.
The instability of the segment is due to the difficulty in determining its center of gravity.
Design a handling sling device that will self-adjust to the load as it is lifted and keep it steady throughout the lifting operation.

impact_notes: Segment suspension failure during transport with 90% of load suspended 10-15 feet above rail car created serious personnel safety hazard; near-miss with potential for catastrophic drop and injury.

title: Loose Access Door Reporting and Securing; Contamination Air Plenum Access Door Hinges
driving_event_text: An access panel located at the aft end of the orbiter just right of the rudder, broke loose from its attaching hardware and the contamination air plenum. The access panel fell and hit the orbiter maneuvering system (OMS) pod handling fixture below. The access panel did not touch the vehicle or the OMS pod. OMS pod removal operations were in progress when the door fell. The door started working loose approximately three weeks prior to the incident, and a trouble call was made. Blue tape was found on the door edges except for the hinged side.
lesson_text: Hinged access panels frequently lose structural integrity near the hinge line and may break off. Pop rivets used to secure access door hinges frequently work their way out leaving the door and hinges dangerously loose.
recommendation_text: Access panel hinge regions that show excessive wear should be repaired upon discovery. Replace all access door rivets with plate nuts and screws. In addition, adding a safety chain/lanyard would further increase the safety margin.

evidence.quote: Pop rivets used to secure access door hinges frequently work their way out leaving the door and hinges dangerously loose.
The door started working loose approximately three weeks prior to the incident, and a trouble call was made. Blue tape was found on the door edges except for the hinged side.
Hinged access panels frequently lose structural integrity near the hinge line and may break off.

impact_notes: Access panel fell during OMS pod removal operations; no direct vehicle damage but created FOD hazard and operational risk. Potential for injury or vehicle damage if panel had struck personnel or critical hardware.

title: Thruster Purge Operations
driving_event_text: During removal of the purge adapter from thruster R4D, liquid MMH was observed leaking from the thruster nozzle. When the leak was discovered the OPF high bay hypergolic exhaust fans were actuated, the high bay evacuated, and fire and medical support were requested to stand by. The leaking MMH was caused by a liquid trap present in the fuel suction hose. The trap was formed by excessive hose length, which was routed to the next level above, then down to the purge adapter. The routing and hose length was left over from a previous operation on the left side of the orbiter.
lesson_text: Failure to require a specific hose route resulted in a liquid trap being inadvertently formed causing liquid MMH to leak from a thruster nozzle.
recommendation_text: Update operational procedures to include a requirement to ensure proper hose configuration and routing prior to beginning purge operations.

evidence.quote: Update operational procedures to include a requirement to ensure proper hose configuration and routing prior to beginning purge operations.
The routing and hose length was left over from a previous operation on the left side of the orbiter.
Failure to require a specific hose route resulted in a liquid trap being inadvertently formed causing liquid MMH to leak from a thruster nozzle.

impact_notes: Hypergolic fuel (MMH) leak required high bay evacuation, emergency fan actuation, and fire/medical standby response. Safety hazard to personnel and facility.

title: OPF Extendable Platforms
driving_event_text: Technicians were in the process of extending platform 10-2 in OPF HB-1. After extending the platform about 3 feet, the observer noted that the platform would not clear the body flap and signaled a "halt." Before the platform could be stopped, it contacted two tiles, damaging them.
lesson_text: Failure to measure and verify adequate clearance, prior to platform extension could result in damage to orbiter thermal protection tiles.
recommendation_text: Review platform extension procedures to ensure verification of ample clearance prior to extending platforms.

evidence.quote: Failure to measure and verify adequate clearance, prior to platform extension could result in damage to orbiter thermal protection tiles.
Review platform extension procedures to ensure verification of ample clearance prior to extending platforms.
the observer noted that the platform would not clear the body flap and signaled a halt. Before the platform could be stopped, it contacted two tiles, damaging them.

impact_notes: Damage to orbiter thermal protection tiles requiring repair; potential schedule impact for tile replacement and re-verification.

title: Lift Operations Utilizing a Hydraset or Dynamometer
driving_event_text: Replacement of the upper floating beam forward roller support shaft was in progress to eliminate a stress corrosion concern. The 5-ton bridge crane and a sling were being used to lift the Pad B PGHM side 2 upper floating beam Z bar off of the Z roller when a small block with two 5/8" bolts apparently sheared, allowing Z Bar to travel up, contacting a portion of the upper floating beam causing the web strap to be cut.
lesson_text: Develop procedures that require the use of hydrasets and dynamometers when lifting unknown amounts of weight.
recommendation_text: Review platform extension procedures to ensure verification of ample clearance prior to extending platforms.

evidence.quote: Develop procedures that require the use of hydrasets and dynamometers when lifting unknown amounts of weight.
a small block with two 5/8" bolts apparently sheared, allowing Z Bar to travel up, contacting a portion of the upper floating beam
Review platform extension procedures to ensure verification of ample clearance prior to extending platforms.

impact_notes: Equipment damage (web strap cut on upper floating beam); operational impact from unplanned equipment failure during maintenance activity; potential safety risk to personnel in lift operations area.

title: Oxygen (O2) Meter Installations
driving_event_text: During rotation of the L/H OMS pod (LP04) an O2 meter was heard rolling inside the pod. The pod was being raised to a vertical position per V5016. Also a "light thud" was heard during the lift. The lift was stopped but it was determined that it would be difficult to remove the meter while it was in this position. The pod was lifted to the vertical position and then the meter was removed.
lesson_text: Lack of safety procedures to document the presence of an O2 meter and constrain movement of the OMS pod resulted in a close call.
recommendation_text: Add a safety call-out to the wad authorizing installation of an O2 meter in flight hardware, constraining movement of hardware with meter installed.

evidence.quote: Lack of safety procedures to document the presence of an O2 meter and constrain movement of the OMS pod resulted in a close call.
During rotation of the L/H OMS pod (LP04) an O2 meter was heard rolling inside the pod.
Add a safety call-out to the wad authorizing installation of an O2 meter in flight hardware, constraining movement of hardware with meter installed.

impact_notes: Close call during pod rotation; potential for meter damage or pod structural damage if movement had continued unchecked; operational delay to safely remove meter from vertical position.

title: Displacement Leak Detector
driving_event_text: A technician was performing leak checks at ports on the right side of the orbiter (OV-099) using a displacement leak detector. The tygon hose became tangled and the leak detector fell and hit the side of the vehicle damaging seven tiles. The detector was damaged when it finally hit the floor. The bay manager, safety, and the operations desk were notified of the incident, and the area was cleaned of spilled fluid & debris.
lesson_text: Failure to require tethering of a flow meter used to perform leak checks resulted in damage to the orbiter when the meter fell and hit the orbiter.
recommendation_text: Add handles and tethering aids to the flow meters prior to use in or around the orbiter.

evidence.quote: Failure to require tethering of a flow meter used to perform leak checks resulted in damage to the orbiter when the meter fell
Add handles and tethering aids to the flow meters prior to use in or around the orbiter
The tygon hose became tangled and the leak detector fell and hit the side of the vehicle damaging seven tiles

impact_notes: Damage to seven orbiter thermal protection system tiles requiring repair; equipment damage; operational disruption for leak check procedures.

title: APU Fuel Isolation Valve Assembly
driving_event_text: An APU hydrazine isolation valve assembly was removed and sent to the shipping section of logistics without being properly packaged for safe handling. In addition, there was no Q.A. check sheet and no decontamination tags with safety buy-off as being sniffed at zero parts per million. There was a conditional decontamination tag with the part indicating that it was from a fuel system, and conditionally decontaminated but absorbed or trapped fuel could out-gas. The OPF was called for safety and technical support to come and check out the valve; the valve tested positive for hydrazine and was immediately safed.
lesson_text: Failure to properly package an APU isolation fuel valve when it was removed from the APU fuel system resulted in a close call.
recommendation_text: Revise/update standard practice instructions (SPI) to include bagging and tagging of components exposed to hypergols prior to transporting them.

evidence.quote: APU hydrazine isolation valve assembly was removed and sent to the shipping section of logistics without being properly packaged for safe handling. There was no Q.A. check sheet and no decontamination tags
Revise/update standard practice instructions (SPI) to include bagging and tagging of components exposed to hypergols prior to transporting them
conditional decontamination tag with the part indicating it was from a fuel system, conditionally decontaminated but absorbed or trapped fuel could out-gas

impact_notes: Close call with hydrazine exposure hazard; valve tested positive for residual hydrazine requiring immediate safing and OPF emergency response.

title: High Pressure Shutoff Valves; Vacuum Pump Isolation Valve
driving_event_text: A vacuum pump isolation valve burst at the Pad B high pressure gas storage battery. The cause of the incident has been attributed to the seepage of pressure from the wheel valves after they were turned off as part of a safing operation. The wheel valve o-ring does not always seat properly. Normally this would not affect the remainder of the system, the seepage pressure would be confined. However, when the vacuum pump was connected to the system, the seepage pressure (1500 psi) was greater than the design capacity of the vacuum pump isolation valve and thus overpressurization occurred.
lesson_text: Failure to establish a preventative maintenance program for two 6000 psi shutoff valves resulted in a close call when they began leaking Failure to use valves capable of withstanding system pressures resulted in a "close call" when a valve exploded.
recommendation_text: Establish a preventative maintenance program for components installed in critical high pressure systems. Ensure that any isolation valve used in a pressure system can withstand the highest pressure of the overall system.

evidence.quote: Failure to use valves capable of withstanding system pressures resulted in a 'close call' when a valve exploded.
The wheel valve o-ring does not always seat properly...seepage pressure (1500 psi) was greater than the design capacity of the vacuum pump isolation valve
Failure to establish a preventative maintenance program for two 6000 psi shutoff valves resulted in a close call when they began leaking

impact_notes: Valve burst at Pad B high pressure gas storage battery; close call with potential for catastrophic failure and facility damage or personnel injury.

title: Draeger Pump
driving_event_text: An engineer was utilizing a draeger pump to perform toxic vapor checks on A/C motor valves on the 207' level of the rotating service structure. When the engineer attempted to retrieve protective apron from the platform, the draeger pump was pulled from the platform and fell, striking the orbiter's FRCS blanket area and landing on the 193' Level.
lesson_text: Failure to secure/tether a draeger pump during toxic vapor checks resulted in a "close call" when the pump fell, striking the orbiter.
recommendation_text: To ensure continued tetherability, provide a permanently attached tether for the draeger pump.

evidence.quote: Failure to secure/tether a draeger pump during toxic vapor checks resulted in a 'close call' when the pump fell, striking the orbiter.
To ensure continued tetherability, provide a permanently attached tether for the draeger pump.

impact_notes: Close call incident with potential for orbiter damage; equipment fell from 207' level striking FRCS blanket area.

title: Pressure System Maintenance
driving_event_text: Performing work on S70-0613-00-001-0120 to change improperly sized flex hose restraints. When attempting removal of flex hose, gaseous nitrogen was released due to a partially opened valve in the system. Isolation valve A102736 on the F70-0014 panel was closed to stop the gaseous nitrogen flow to the flex hose. The gaseous nitrogen contained small quantities of N2H4 vapors. The work authorizing document did not contain instructions or work stops to verify that there was no pressure on the flex hose.
lesson_text: Work authorizing document failed to include instructions/work stops to verify that there was no pressure on the system; a "close call" resulted.
recommendation_text: System engineers should update work authorizing documents to include verification of zero pressure on systems prior to making repairs.

evidence.quote: Work authorizing document did not contain instructions or work stops to verify that there was no pressure on the flex hose.
Work authorizing document failed to include instructions/work stops to verify that there was no pressure on the system
System engineers should update work authorizing documents to include verification of zero pressure on systems prior to making repairs.

impact_notes: Gaseous nitrogen release containing N2H4 vapors during maintenance work; close call with potential personnel safety hazard and system damage.

title: OMS Engine Nozzle Cover
driving_event_text: During orbiter lift to vertical in the vab transfer isle between high Bays 1 and 2, the right hand OMS engine nozzle cover fell approximately 15 feet to the floor. The cover suffered enough damage to prevent it from being reinstalled. The cover was found to have three holding clamps that were probably defective on installation at the OPF. The left hand cover was inspected and several loose clamps were found and were tightened. Safety gave the ok to continue the lift without the cover.
lesson_text: Lack of a preventative maintenance program for the OMS engine nozzle covers resulted in a "close call" when the covers' holding clamps failed.
recommendation_text: Evaluate OMS engine nozzle cover holding clamps to determine whether redesign or preventative maintenance should be implemented.

evidence.quote: Lack of a preventative maintenance program for the OMS engine nozzle covers resulted in a "close call" when the covers' holding clamps failed.
The cover was found to have three holding clamps that were probably defective on installation at the OPF.
Safety gave the ok to continue the lift without the cover.

impact_notes: Cover fell 15 feet and suffered damage preventing reinstallation; safety risk during orbiter transfer operations; potential for injury or vehicle damage.

title: Radiographic Inspections
driving_event_text: Technicians were performing radiographic inspection of the insulation area in the atlas stage thrust section utilizing a 100KV machine. The tubehead was placed inside the thrust area taped into position and exposures were made to the outside. The tape failed to hold the tubehead and it slipped down 6-8 inches hitting a threaded area on a strut. There was only visible damage to the top of 2-3 threads.
lesson_text: Heat from hot weather caused the tape used to secure a tubehead assembly to soften allowing the tubehead to fall, resulting in a "close call".
recommendation_text: Update documentation to require technicians to use rope, web straps or something of that nature in addition to tape to secure the tubehead assembly.

evidence.quote: Heat from hot weather caused the tape used to secure a tubehead assembly to soften allowing the tubehead to fall
Update documentation to require technicians to use rope, web straps or something of that nature in addition to tape

impact_notes: Close call with potential equipment damage; threaded strut sustained visible damage to 2-3 threads but no critical failure occurred.

title: LH2 Leakage at the 8" T-0 Flexhose QD/Interface
driving_event_text: The 8 inch T-0 flexhose connects the tail service mast (TSM) with the LH2 carrier plate. Due to mechanical design the interface connection is very poor. The LO2 interface is identical and has experienced leakage in the past. Undetected leakage could cause a major fire resulting loss of life or vehicle.
lesson_text: Lack of adequate LH2 Leak detection at the 8 inch T-0 flexhose QD/interface could result in fire.
recommendation_text: Design/install a new leak detector sensor console to accommodate additional leak detectors.

evidence.quote: Due to mechanical design the interface connection is very poor. The LO2 interface is identical and has experienced leakage in the past.
Lack of adequate LH2 Leak detection at the 8 inch T-0 flexhose QD/interface could result in fire.
Undetected leakage could cause a major fire resulting loss of life or vehicle.

impact_notes: Potential loss of life and vehicle due to major fire from undetected cryogenic leakage at critical ground support interface.

title: Gaseous Helium Injection During Slow LOX Fill
driving_event_text: The helium injection system introduces a controlled flow of helium into the aft end of the feedline to prevent geysers during propellant loading and holds before launch. The helium is supplied from panel S72-0697-08 through one series valve (A78410) and two parallel valves. Failure of the series valve or clogging of the downstream filter could cause loss of bubbling. Loss of helium bubbling prior to 2% full or after 98% full in the external tank (ET) could result in damage due to geysering.
lesson_text: Failure of the helium injection valve or clogging of the filter could cause loss of the helium injection system.
recommendation_text: Develop a preventative maintenance program for the critical valves and filters in this system.

evidence.quote: Failure of the series valve or clogging of the downstream filter could cause loss of bubbling. Loss of helium bubbling prior to 2% full or after 98% full...could result in damage due to geysering.
Develop a preventative maintenance program for the critical valves and filters in this system.

impact_notes: Loss of helium injection system could result in external tank damage due to geysering during propellant loading, risking mission safety and vehicle integrity.

title: Vacuum Read-out Stations for the MLP's LH2 system
driving_event_text: The LH2 system maintenance includes a monthly vacuum reading in the annulus space in the overhead vacuum-jacketed (VJ) high point bleed and LH2 fill lines. Access to the read-out ports in the GH2 fill line on side 1 of the MLPS is precarious. There are not adequate tie-off points for body harness and lanyards. The readout ports are 10-12 feet above the floor grating. If a technician uses a ladder for access, or climbs on existing structures to make the reading, he/she is within inches of exterior handrails and above them. This places him/her about 40 feet above the vab floor or the pad surface.
lesson_text: Inadequate access to the vacuum read-out stations on the MLP LH2 system's vacuum jacketed lines could lead to personnel injury.
recommendation_text: Relocate the vacuum read-out stations to an area and height convenient and safe for technicians to easily access.

evidence.quote: Access to the read-out ports in the GH2 fill line on side 1 of the MLPS is precarious. There are not adequate tie-off points for body harness and lanyards.

If a technician uses a ladder for access, or climbs on existing structures to make the reading, he/she is within inches of exterior handrails and above them. This places him/her about 40 feet above the vab floor or the pad surface.
Inadequate access to the vacuum read-out stations on the MLP LH2 system's vacuum jacketed lines could lead to personnel injury.

impact_notes: Personnel safety hazard: technicians required to access read-out ports at 40 feet elevation with inadequate fall protection and tie-off points, creating risk of serious injury or fatality.

title: LH2 Quick Disconnects (QD)
driving_event_text: Quick disconnects, utilized for providing gasses to the space shuttle main engine drying/purge lines and the main propulsion system LH2 prepressurization system are identical and can be cross-connected in the OPF. The MPS heated GN2 purge panel provides heated or unheated GN2 at 645 +/- 65 psig for engine drying. Should GN2 645 +/- 65 psig be introduced to the GH2 prepressurization system, flight hardware damage could occur. This condition may or may not be visible on the firing room pressure gages depending upon the status of orbiter power.
lesson_text: Quick disconnects utilized for providing gasses to the space shuttle main engine (SSME) are identical to each other and can be cross-connected.
recommendation_text: Modify (KEY) one quick disconnect to prevent misconnection.

evidence.quote: Quick disconnects utilized for providing gasses to the space shuttle main engine (SSME) are identical to each other and can be cross-connected.
Should GN2 645 +/- 65 psig be introduced to the GH2 prepressurization system, flight hardware damage could occur.
This condition may or may not be visible on the firing room pressure gages depending upon the status of orbiter power.

impact_notes: Potential flight hardware damage to LH2 prepressurization system if wrong gas introduced; condition may be undetectable in real-time depending on orbiter power status, creating latent failure risk.

title: Space Shuttle Main Engine (SSME) Vertical Installer
driving_event_text: The SSME vertical installer is used to position the engine for installation and removal. Failure of this equipment to maintain positive control could lead to unexpected movement. During visual inspection of the air piston and bore of the vertical installer, it was discovered that the air piston had a scored landring. This condition occurs when the piston sticks in the cylinder (due to lack of lubrication and/or foreign material in the cylinder). Pressure then builds up until the piston breaks free. When the piston breaks free it can travel to the stops with enough momentum to cause sudden uncontrolled movement of the engine.
lesson_text: Lack of a preventative maintenance program for the vertical installer resulted in failure of the installer to maintain control over the SSME.
recommendation_text: Develop and implement a preventative maintenance program for the SSME vertical installer.

evidence.quote: Lack of a preventative maintenance program for the vertical installer resulted in failure of the installer to maintain control over the SSME.
This condition occurs when the piston sticks in the cylinder (due to lack of lubrication and/or foreign material in the cylinder).
Develop and implement a preventative maintenance program for the SSME vertical installer.

impact_notes: Failure to maintain positive control of SSME during installation/removal could result in uncontrolled engine movement, creating serious personnel safety hazard and potential damage to flight hardware.

title: The Digital Computer Unit Memory Loader
driving_event_text: The memory and registers of the main engine controller digital computer unit interface must be loaded and read and must provide discrete controls and status indicators compatible with a punched tape reader and teletypewriter interface for automatic program loading. Inadequate grounding of the DCU memory loader (i.e., Ground cables not connected, connected improperly, or in deteriorated condition) could result in electric shock injury to personnel.
lesson_text: Inadequate grounding of the digital computer unit (DCU) memory loader can cause electrical shock injury to personnel.
recommendation_text: Update OMI to include a ground verification test of the DCU memory loader prior to beginning operations.

evidence.quote: Inadequate grounding of the DCU memory loader (i.e., Ground cables not connected, connected improperly, or in deteriorated condition) could result in electric shock injury to personnel.
Update OMI to include a ground verification test of the DCU memory loader prior to beginning operations.

impact_notes: Personnel safety hazard: electrical shock injury risk from inadequate grounding of DCU memory loader equipment.

title: Payload Bay Door (PBD) Opening/Closing Operations
driving_event_text: During OMI V9023.F operations, the L/H payload bay door was noticed to have a slight upward bend in it due to excessive force placed on it through the L/H AFT PBD support jack. The aft half of the L/H PBD was raised eight to ten inches higher than the position of the PBD forward segment. An interference problem occurs when trying to insert/remove the "C" frame support hook from the support jack dowel. The interference problem requires the use of more than one technician to force the "C" frame support hook on and off the support jack dowel.
lesson_text: The "C" frame support hook and support jack dowel require manual manipulation and experience interference during PBD opening/closing operations. PBD damage may occur due to excessive forces incurred on it by jacking up PBD until its weight forces "C" hook to engage with support jack dowel.
recommendation_text: Redesign the "C" frame support hook/support jack dowel to engage/disengage smoothly with use of only jack to raise/lower PBD jack support dowel. Modify PBD support jacks to allow smooth engagement/disengagement of "C" frame support hook during PBD opening/closing operations.

evidence.quote: The "C" frame support hook and support jack dowel require manual manipulation and experience interference during PBD opening/closing operations.
PBD damage may occur due to excessive forces incurred on it by jacking up PBD until its weight forces "C" hook to engage with support jack dowel.
The aft half of the L/H PBD was raised eight to ten inches higher than the position of the PBD forward segment.

impact_notes: Payload bay door structural damage requiring redesign of support mechanism; operational risk during critical ground operations.

title: External Tank (ET) and Solid Rocket Booster (SRB) Demating Operations
driving_event_text: While demating the ET from the SRB's during OMI S0033, TPS foam damage occurred on the +Y forward crossover area. Scraping noises were heard and interference was observed. The operation was stopped while a deviation to the OMI was written to allow north movement of the crane in an attempt to relieve the interference. The operation proceeded but foam damage was unavoidable due to insufficient clearance. This insufficient clearance was due to incorrect installation of support brackets caused by inaccurate OMI procedures.
lesson_text: Improper installation of support brackets creates insufficient clearance between ET and SRB's during demating operations.
recommendation_text: Update OMI to reflect correct support bracket installation, including caution of clearance limitations if improperly installed.

evidence.quote: insufficient clearance was due to incorrect installation of support brackets caused by inaccurate OMI procedures
Scraping noises were heard and interference was observed. The operation was stopped while a deviation to the OMI was written
foam damage was unavoidable due to insufficient clearance

impact_notes: TPS foam damage to External Tank during demating operations; required deviation and operational workaround; potential impact to vehicle readiness and schedule.

title: Helicopter Hydraulic Line Chafing
driving_event_text: Immediately after becoming airborne at the SLF, the pilot of a KSC NASA helicopter noticed a failure in the No.1 hydraulic system (which supports helicopter flight controls). The loss of this system is an emergency. An inspection was conducted on the hydraulic system which revealed the line was approx. 0.5 inches shorter than required for optimal operation. Due to the curvature of the line passing through the spacer block, the line was not seated flush in its hole. The spacer block, with rotor vibration, caused the hydraulic line to experience strand separation, resulting in a puncture.
lesson_text: Vibration during helicopter rotor operation may cause shortened hydraulic lines to become rub-fatigued/chafed at the spacer block contact points.
recommendation_text: Periodically inspect flight control lines and spacer blocks for chafing and replace as necessary, ensuring that proper size lines are used.

evidence.quote: the line was approx. 0.5 inches shorter than required for optimal operation
Vibration during helicopter rotor operation may cause shortened hydraulic lines to become rub-fatigued/chafed at the spacer block contact points
Periodically inspect flight control lines and spacer blocks for chafing and replace as necessary

impact_notes: Loss of No.1 hydraulic system supporting helicopter flight controls constitutes an emergency condition with potential for catastrophic flight safety impact.

title: Mobile Launch Platform (MLP) Air-arcing Operation
driving_event_text: During air-arcing operations under mobile launch platform-1 (MLP), a welder smelled smoke through the hole he was working on and found a fire in the floor insulation of compartment 16B. Investigation revealed that air-arcing under MLP-1 ignited hydraulic fluid that had accumulated in the floor insulation of compartment 16B. Hydraulic pumping units leaked hydraulic fluid onto the insulation for several years. Hot particles released by air-arcing blew into compartment 16B as the welder penetrated the MLP bottom panel, and the fluid ignited.
lesson_text: Hydraulic fluid, dripped by hydraulic pumping units onto floor insulation may be ignited by hot particles propelled into the area during air-arcing.
recommendation_text: Design and install drain pans to catch and contain leaking hydraulic fluid beneath hydraulic components.

evidence.quote: Hydraulic pumping units leaked hydraulic fluid onto the insulation for several years.
Hot particles released by air-arcing blew into compartment 16B as the welder penetrated the MLP bottom panel, and the fluid ignited.
Design and install drain pans to catch and contain leaking hydraulic fluid beneath hydraulic components.

impact_notes: Fire ignition during critical launch platform operations; potential for personnel injury and mission delay.

title: Solid Rocket Motor (SRM) Segment and Handling Ring Mating
driving_event_text: On September 25, 1987, The solid rocket motor (SRM) aft segment was being mated to the aft segment handling ring in the H77-0386 stand. The SRM segment and handling ring assembly was hoisted from the stand and moved to the breakover beam. The handling ring was mated to the breakover beam and the SRM segment was tilted to the horizontal position and placed on the rail car. At this time it was noticed that a total of six bolts were missing from the SRM end-ring assembly. It is unknown when or who removed the bolts.
lesson_text: Documentation lacking cross references between serial numbers and model numbers may result in inadequate configuration or missing hardware. Improper work authorization contributed to the unauthorized removal of six attachment bolts from the SRM aft segment lifting bracket.
recommendation_text: Implement documentation to allow work to be traced to hardware serial numbers and personnel performing the task. Revise documentation to include hardware inspections and to correct discrepencies for all transportation support equipment transferred.

evidence.quote: It is unknown when or who removed the bolts.
Documentation lacking cross references between serial numbers and model numbers may result in inadequate configuration or missing hardware.
Improper work authorization contributed to the unauthorized removal of six attachment bolts

impact_notes: Six critical attachment bolts missing from SRM aft segment lifting bracket discovered during assembly operations; potential for catastrophic structural failure during handling and transport if not detected.

title: Liquid Tanker (LT) Liquid Level Indicator (LLI) Reliability, Float Assembly Movement, and Float Assembly Specific Gravity (SG)
driving_event_text: On July 13, 1988, hydrazine was being transferred from partially filled drums into LT-44. There were seven drums for a total of 343 gallons. Adding the 134 gallons in LT-44 resulted in 477 gallons - well within the tank's 500 gallon capacity. As the hydrazine was being transferred from the last drum, a mechanic saw liquid spray from the vent hose and immediately closed the vent and fill valves. After securing the area, mechanics reported that all the flags at the top half of the LLI indicated the tanker was full. This suggested the magnetic liquid level gauge's reliability is questionable due to erratic operations.
lesson_text: Shocks/vibrations induced into a magnetic liquid level gauge may affect the position of individual flag indicators and lead to erroneous readings. Fluid contamination may cause a restriction of the float assembly movement within the chamber, leading to an inaccurate reading. Fluid in the LT having a SG which is less than the float assembly is weighted for, indicates a fluid level lower than actual, and may lead to a tank overfill.
recommendation_text: Redesign flags to lock and remain stationary (until acted upon by external force) as fluid raises magnetic float to interact with each flag magnet. Filter all fluid loaded in LT's to eliminate particulate contamination which may collect on chamber walls. Install float assemblies weighted to a fluid SG of approx. 1.0 so fluid concentrations will register maximum level and avoid tank over filling.

evidence.quote: Shocks/vibrations induced into a magnetic liquid level gauge may affect the position of individual flag indicators and lead to erroneous readings.
Fluid contamination may cause a restriction of the float assembly movement within the chamber, leading to an inaccurate reading.
Fluid in the LT having a SG which is less than the float assembly is weighted for, indicates a fluid level lower than actual, and may lead to a tank overfill.

impact_notes: Tank overfill event with hydrazine (toxic propellant) resulted in liquid spray from vent hose; potential for personnel exposure and environmental contamination.

title: Orbiter Vehicle (OV) Hydrogen (H2) Tank Removal
driving_event_text: During removal of the H2 tank from the OV-104 mid-body, a crane was used to take up the initial cable slack and transfer the load from the tank struts to the GSE lifting fixture. The move director called for a crane movement of "up at 1 in/sec" and the crane controller relayed the command to the crane operator. The actual lift rate was on the order of "up at 3 in/sec" and a momentary vertical load of 800 lbs was applied to the tank assembly. This load was immediately reduced and the tank bolts removed. The normal transfer load is made up of a 150 lb tank lifting fixture and a 235 lb tank weight.
lesson_text: Cranes used to remove slack and transfer loads from tank struts to a ground support equipment lifting fixture may cause a momentary vertical overload.
recommendation_text: Update OMI procedure to utilize hydraset units (in place of cranes) during transferring of loads to avoid overloading tank assemblies.

evidence.quote: The move director called for a crane movement of "up at 1 in/sec" and the crane controller relayed the command to the crane operator. The actual lift rate was on the order of "up at 3 in/sec"

a momentary vertical load of 800 lbs was applied to the tank assembly. This load was immediately reduced and the tank bolts removed. The normal transfer load is made up of a 150 lb tank lifting fixture and a 235 lb tank weight.
Update OMI procedure to utilize hydraset units (in place of cranes) during transferring of loads to avoid overloading tank assemblies.

impact_notes: Momentary 800 lb overload (3.4× nominal 235 lb tank weight) applied to tank assembly during critical removal operation; potential for structural damage or failure if load had been sustained.

title: Slack Removal from Hoisting Sling
driving_event_text: Task leader started hoisting operation with a "slow micro" command to take up sling slack until 337 lbs was obtained. A joystick controls hoist movement which may be controlled at a low rate, but only by operator judgement. A switch energizes the hoist motor and disengages the brakes when depressed. If the joystick is in the null position when the button is depressed, load movement will occur with speed dependent on stick position when the brake is released. An overload at 900 lbs in the IPS resulted when "slow micro" was requested and the brake button was depressed with the joystick not in the null position.
lesson_text: The speed associated with crane commands is a matter of operator judgement and may result in an overload to the instrument pointing system (IPS).
recommendation_text: Utilize hydrasets to apply a desired load at a predetermined speed to the IPS during hoisting operations.

evidence.quote: The speed associated with crane commands is a matter of operator judgement and may result in an overload to the instrument pointing system (IPS).
If the joystick is in the null position when the button is depressed, load movement will occur with speed dependent on stick position when the brake is released.
An overload at 900 lbs in the IPS resulted when 'slow micro' was requested and the brake button was depressed with the joystick not in the null position.

impact_notes: Overload condition (900 lbs vs. intended 337 lbs) to instrument pointing system; potential equipment damage and mission impact.

title: Loads Imposed on Stabilizing Frame During Mate/demate Operations
driving_event_text: The H70-0743 sling assembly consists of a forward and aft spreader beam assembly connected together by a segmented stabilizing frame assembly. With the two mobile crane concept, the sling assembly is used to lift and demate the orbiter to the shuttle carrier aircraft. At the contingency landing site, the two mobile crane concept is used with a 800-ton T-C 4000 truck crane for the aft lift and a 250-ton link belt for the forward lift. Visual observation must be maintained at all times during hoisting operations due to extremely limited clearances between the orbiter and facilities.
lesson_text: Excessive loads may be imposed on the stabilizing frame assembly while hoisting during mate/demate operations.
recommendation_text: Maintain crane lift cables vertical and sling assembly level to prevent excessive loads imposed on stabilizing frame center segment.

evidence.quote: Visual observation must be maintained at all times during hoisting operations due to extremely limited clearances between the orbiter and facilities.
Excessive loads may be imposed on the stabilizing frame assembly while hoisting during mate/demate operations.
Maintain crane lift cables vertical and sling assembly level to prevent excessive loads imposed on stabilizing frame center segment.

impact_notes: Risk of structural failure to stabilizing frame during critical orbiter mate/demate operations; potential for orbiter damage or safety hazard to personnel and facilities.

title: Orbiter Compartment Overpressurization
driving_event_text: The portable purge unit supplies conditioned air through ducts to the orbiter to purge and/or pressurize the payload bay's forward and aft compartments. Airflow varies between 50 lbs/min minimum to 100 lbs/min maximum. Vent door observers are always on station when a vent door is repositioned for a test or TPS tile/thermal barrier work. Overpressurization may occur if all the orbiter vent doors (normally in the purge position) for a compartment are closed at one time. Control logic program VAM09 also notifies the firing room engineers if both a right hand and left hand purge vent door set is closed.
lesson_text: Normal purge air applied to the orbiter compartment while the vent doors are closed may cause major orbiter damage.
recommendation_text: Specify in work document that vent doors must remain in standard purge configuration and all on-board vent door circuit breakers remain "open".

evidence.quote: Vent door observers are always on station when a vent door is repositioned for a test or TPS tile/thermal barrier work.
Normal purge air applied to the orbiter compartment while the vent doors are closed may cause major orbiter damage.
Specify in work document that vent doors must remain in standard purge configuration and all on-board vent door circuit breakers remain 'open'.

impact_notes: Overpressurization could cause major orbiter structural damage. Incident prevented by procedural controls and observer presence, but underlying risk required formalized work document specification.

title: Orbiter Protection System "Y" Curtain Wall Retraction
driving_event_text: During the retraction of the orbiter protection system "Y" curtain wall in the console mode, the speed attained after leaving the slow travel transition is excessive. The operator must manipulate a manual valve to reduce air flow to the winch. High impact stops from the rapid movement of the "Y" curtain wall during retraction may cause personnel injury and/or damage to payloads and facility (PAD).
lesson_text: Payloads and facility damage and/or personnel injury may occur due to high impact stops of "Y" curtain wall during retraction.
recommendation_text: Install and utilize a flow control valve in the air winch supply line to permit speed regulation of the winch in the console mode.

evidence.quote: the speed attained after leaving the slow travel transition is excessive. The operator must manipulate a manual valve to reduce air flow
High impact stops from the rapid movement of the "Y" curtain wall during retraction may cause personnel injury and/or damage to payloads and facility
Install and utilize a flow control valve in the air winch supply line to permit speed regulation

impact_notes: Personnel injury risk and potential damage to payloads and facility infrastructure from uncontrolled high-speed retraction impacts.

title: Main Landing Gear (MLG) Tire Rupture During Postlanding Operations
driving_event_text: MLG tire heat generated from max. Braking due to a crosswind landing, heavy orbiter, or long landing, could cause a transfer of heat to continue to the MLG tires after wheel stop, resulting in a delayed tire blowout. An explosive tire rupture caused by increasing tire pressure, cuts, excessive loading, or skidding could injure personnel and/or damage the orbiter/chassis hydraulic system/ground support equipment. Three thermal relief plugs in each MLG tire prevent tire overpressurization, the orbiter anti-skid/brake control system prevents locked brakes, and the orbiter hydraulic system displacement limiter limits hydraulic fluid loss.
lesson_text: Transfer of heat from brakes to the MLG tires after maximum braking could increase tire pressure and result in rupture/blowout. Excessive tire wear due to maximum braking or FOD may reduce strength of tire structure and cause rise in tire pressure, resulting in rupture. Hydraulic fluid leakage in the hot wheel well area may ignite and cause orbiter damage and/or personnel injury.
recommendation_text: To minimize possibility of improper braking, provide crew training with braking techniques for various landing profiles. Prior to orbiter leaving the OPF, perform anti-skid brake inspections and check electrical power/pedal calibration integrity and hydraulic control valves. A fire fighting capability is mandatory at all planned landing sites should a hydraulic fire occur after wheel stop.

evidence.quote: Transfer of heat from brakes to the MLG tires after maximum braking could increase tire pressure and result in rupture/blowout.
provide crew training with braking techniques for various landing profiles. Prior to orbiter leaving the OPF, perform anti-skid brake inspections
Excessive tire wear due to maximum braking or FOD may reduce strength of tire structure and cause rise in tire pressure, resulting in rupture.

impact_notes: Explosive tire rupture could injure personnel and damage orbiter hydraulic system, ground support equipment, and cause hydraulic fire in hot wheel well area.

title: Environmental Control System (ECS) Pressure Distribution Unit
driving_event_text: The environmental control system (ECS) pressure distribution unit pre-operations set-up requires several flexhose connections and leak checks prior to operation. Pressure lines could be misconnected and result in damage to flight hardware or injury to personnel.
lesson_text: Similar adjacent connections on the ECS pressure distribution system allow for the possibility of misconnected lines resulting in damage.
recommendation_text: Install keyed connectors to prevent misconnection of pressure lines.

evidence.quote: Similar adjacent connections on the ECS pressure distribution system allow for the possibility of misconnected lines resulting in damage.
Pressure lines could be misconnected and result in damage to flight hardware or injury to personnel.
Install keyed connectors to prevent misconnection of pressure lines.

impact_notes: Potential for flight hardware damage and personnel injury from misconnected pressure lines during pre-operations setup.

title: Power Reactant Storage and Distribution (PRSD) Pod Latching Mechanism
driving_event_text: The QD/filter assemblies are designed to provide a disconnected interface and remove all solid particles from the servicing fluids, which exceed 25 microns in size. The system supports the required operating pressures, contaminant capacities and rated flows. A scupper to support the pod has not been provided for Dryden Flight Research Center. This mishap was caused by a worn or deformed stop pin in the pod latching mechanism. The worn pin allows the latching dog to rotate past the mate position to the demate position. Failure has occurred on several occasions.
lesson_text: Failure to establish a preventative maintenance program for the PRSD pod latching mechanism has resulted in damage to the thermal protection system.
recommendation_text: Establish a preventative maintenance program for the PRSD pod latching mechanism.

evidence.quote: Failure to establish a preventative maintenance program for the PRSD pod latching mechanism has resulted in damage to the thermal protection system.
This mishap was caused by a worn or deformed stop pin in the pod latching mechanism. Failure has occurred on several occasions.
A scupper to support the pod has not been provided for Dryden Flight Research Center.

impact_notes: Damage to thermal protection system from repeated latching mechanism failures due to worn stop pins; multiple failure occurrences indicate systemic operational risk.

title: Power Reactant Storage and Distribution (PRSD) LH2/GH2 Fuel Cell Servicing
driving_event_text: The orbiter PRSD system provides GH2/GO2 for fuel cell operation. The PRSD H2 T-0 umbilical vent is installed to support controlled venting of the PRSD H2 system during convoy operations. The umbilical vent is connected to the backpressure relief panel by flexhoses. Pressure must be maintained on the PRSD manifold during fuel cell cool down until vent stack and condenser exit temperatures are within 15 degrees Fahrenheit of ambient. Fixed hydrogen leak detection sensors monitor the fuel cell servicing/deservicing system areas for H2 leaks which could result in damage to vehicle/facility or personnel injury.
lesson_text: A PRSD GH2 leak may result in accumulation of flammable vapors in orbiter compartments or near umbilicals, causing injury or damage.
recommendation_text: Provide periodic inspection/preventative maintenance on fixed hydrogen leak detection sensors and thoroughly inspect for flammable/toxic vapors.
